prompt: Legionella risk in site temporary water systems is controlled by:

options.text: Never flushing unused outlets for months
Avoiding stagnant water, temperature control, flushing, and competent water system management
Storing warm water in hoses in full sun only
Assuming biological risk applies only to hospitals

explanation: Stagnant warm water supports Legionella; site welfare tanks and hoses need management like permanent systems.

distractorExplanations.c: Warm stagnant hoses are high risk.
distractorExplanations.d: Legionella can affect any poorly managed water system.

prompt: A farmer enters a grain silo to clear a blockage while auger is energised. The critical errors include:

options.text: Wearing hi-vis vest only
Working during daylight hours only
Confined space engulfment/mechanical hazards without isolation, atmosphere test, and permit
Using a wooden shovel exclusively

explanation: Silo entry involves engulfment, dust explosion, and machinery risks — isolation and confined space procedures are essential.

distractorExplanations.a: Hi-vis does not address engulfment or auger.
distractorExplanations.b: Hi-vis does not address engulfment or auger.
distractorExplanations.d: Tool material alone does not control major risks.

prompt: Which SDS section is most useful for selecting emergency spill response?

options.text: Section on transport advertising slogans only
Section listing competitor products
Section on office desk ergonomics
Section on accidental release measures and fire-fighting measures

explanation: SDS accidental release and fire sections guide containment, PPE, and incompatibilities during emergencies.

distractorExplanations.a: Marketing slogans are not in SDS spill guidance.
distractorExplanations.b: Marketing slogans are not in SDS spill guidance.
distractorExplanations.c: Competitor lists are irrelevant to response.

prompt: Fall through a fragile skylight during roof maintenance is prevented best by:

options.text: Using marked crawl boards, barriers, or working from below/alternative access avoiding the fragile panel
Walking on unmarked glass panels to distribute weight evenly
Leaning over fragile panels while tethered incorrectly
Removing skylights without edge protection on the opening

explanation: Fragile surfaces need identification, barriers, covers, or avoidance — classic IGC work at height scenario.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Incorrect tethering may not prevent fall through.

prompt: Exposure to blood on sharp contaminated waste requires:

options.text: Squeezing waste into general bin to save cost
No hand contact, sharps container, training on biological routes, and incident reporting for potential BBV exposure
Mouth pipetting in waste area
Ignoring splash because uniform looks clean

explanation: Sharps and bloodborne virus risk need segregation, PPE, and post-exposure protocols.

distractorExplanations.a: General waste mixing increases injury and infection risk.
distractorExplanations.c: Mouth pipetting is prohibited in labs and waste contexts.
distractorExplanations.d: Invisible contamination still poses BBV risk.

prompt: A COSHH inventory helps an organisation by:

options.text: Hiding substances from regulators during visits
Replacing risk assessments entirely
Listing substances used, locations, quantities, and linking to assessments and SDS
Eliminating the need for LEV maintenance

explanation: Inventory supports assessment review, emergency planning, and audit of hazardous substances on site.

distractorExplanations.d: LEV still needs maintenance and test.

prompt: Trip on trailing extension cable in a workshop is reduced by:

options.text: Running cables diagonally across main doors
Coiling excess cable loosely in walkways
Increasing cable count without management
Overhead routing, cable covers in walkways, cordless tools where feasible, and housekeeping rules

explanation: Cable management is a standard STF control in workshops and events.

distractorExplanations.a: Doorways are high traffic; cables there increase trips.

prompt: Lone working policy should define:

options.text: High-risk tasks prohibited alone, check-in intervals, escalation if contact lost, and training
Unlimited solo confined space entry for efficiency
No communication technology provided
Penalty if worker requests assistance

explanation: Policies set prohibited tasks, communication, and emergency escalation for lone workers.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Communication is core to lone worker safety.

prompt: LEV statutory thorough examination is required because:

options.text: LEV eliminates all chemical hazards permanently without maintenance
Performance can degrade invisibly while workers believe they are protected
Examination replaces operator training
Filters never clog on dusty processes

explanation: Thorough examination verifies airflow and capture; degraded LEV gives false confidence.

distractorExplanations.a: LEV reduces exposure but needs maintenance; not all hazards eliminated.

prompt: Work at height using ladders is acceptable when:

options.text: Always preferred over scaffolding for any task
Used horizontally as a platform between two trucks
Risk assessment shows low duration/light work and ladders are secured, inspected, and used by trained staff
Three workers on one ladder for speed

explanation: Ladders are for short-duration light work where risk assessment justifies; misuse as platforms or overcrowding is unsafe.

distractorExplanations.a: Scaffold or MEWP may be required for longer/heavier tasks.

prompt: Welfare on a demolition site should consider:

options.text: Single toilet for 150 workers without maintenance
Eating in the demolition zone to save walking time
No drinking water to reduce toilet need
Dusty work requiring washing, hearing rest areas, potable water, and separate clean eating space

explanation: Demolition welfare must address dust, noise, hydration, and hygiene with maintained facilities.

distractorExplanations.b: Eating in demolition zone increases ingestion of dust.
distractorExplanations.c: Eating in demolition zone increases ingestion of dust.

prompt: A biological spill in a clinic procedure room requires:

options.text: Absorb with appropriate kit, disinfect per protocol, PPE, waste segregation, and exposure review for staff
Mop into corridor without disinfectant
Ignore if spill is small because viruses are harmless
Compress waste into personal bag for home disposal

explanation: Biological spill procedures protect staff and others via disinfectant, PPE, and clinical waste routes.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.d: Clinical waste must use approved containers.

prompt: COSHH substitution means:

options.text: Using a more toxic chemical because it is cheaper
Replacing a hazardous substance or process with a less hazardous one where reasonably practicable
Removing all labels to simplify storage
Substituting training with hope

explanation: Substitution is high in COSHH hierarchy — less hazardous products or processes reduce exposure potential.

distractorExplanations.a: Cheaper more toxic choice contradicts substitution.
distractorExplanations.c: Labels are required for hazard communication.
distractorExplanations.d: Training complements but does not substitute substances.

prompt: Fall from a mezzanine edge where pallets overhang the void is controlled by:

options.text: Overhang pallets for forklift convenience without barriers
Remove signage to reduce visual clutter
Edge protection, pallet placement rules, signage, and supervision of loading activity
Allow pedestrians under loading area without exclusion

explanation: Mezzanine edges need guarding and safe loading practices to prevent falls and dropped object strikes.

distractorExplanations.a: Overhang without protection increases fall and drop risk.

prompt: Monitoring personal exposure to isocyanate paint mist may involve:

options.text: Taste testing the paint for sweetness
Assuming PPE means no measurement needed ever
Measuring only warehouse temperature
Air sampling, biological monitoring where appropriate, and comparison with WEL

explanation: Isocyanates are potent sensitisers; exposure monitoring validates control effectiveness beyond assuming PPE works.

distractorExplanations.a: Taste testing is unsafe and invalid.
distractorExplanations.b: PPE failure and skin routes still need assessment.
distractorExplanations.c: PPE failure and skin routes still need assessment.

prompt: Slip risk in a commercial kitchen is increased by:

options.text: Grease on floors, wet surfaces, poor footwear policy, and inadequate cleaning schedule
Non-slip footwear policy with cleaning regime
Grease traps maintained regularly
Dry mop of spillages during service

explanation: Kitchen STF risks come from grease/water and footwear; controls include cleaning, mats, and suitable shoes.

distractorExplanations.c: Grease trap maintenance limits floor contamination.

prompt: Confined space 'permit issuer' must verify:

options.text: Only that paperwork colour matches folder
Isolation, atmospheric tests, rescue arrangements, and competence before signing
That entry can proceed without gas detector calibration
That lunch break was skipped

explanation: Permit issuers confirm precautions are live — not merely paperwork completion.

distractorExplanations.d: Meal breaks relate to welfare, not permit technical checks.

prompt: A lone engineer visits domestic boilers in tenants' homes. Risk controls include:

options.text: Hiding identity to surprise tenants
No appointment system to increase unpredictability
Lone worker traceability, violence/assault awareness, safe entry procedures, and mobile communication
Cash-only payments in unsafe locations without policy

explanation: Domestic lone work faces variable environments and violence risk; traceability and training are key.

distractorExplanations.d: Cash handling policy should minimise robbery risk.

prompt: Storage of flammable solvents in a COSHH cupboard should follow:

options.text: Storing next to welding bay without firewall
Keeping oxidisers on the same shelf unseparated
Decanting into unlabeled drink bottles
Ventilated, fire-rated store, quantity limits, segregation from oxidisers, and no ignition sources nearby

explanation: Flammable storage needs rated cabinets/rooms, segregation, ventilation, and ignition source control.

distractorExplanations.a: Welding ignition near bulk solvent is catastrophic risk.

prompt: Work at height rescue plan should:

options.text: Be planned before work starts, with trained personnel and equipment suited to the suspension scenario
Be improvised only after a fall occurs
Rely on the fallen person self-rescuing always
Exclude emergency services from planning

explanation: Rescue plans address suspension trauma and timely recovery — required in work at height planning.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Suspended workers may be unconscious; self-rescue not guaranteed.
distractorExplanations.d: Emergency services may be part of the plan.

prompt: Housekeeping standard on a busy factory floor reduces:

options.text: Only office paper recycling rates
Slips, trips, fires, and struck-by incidents from clutter and waste
Psychosocial stress exclusively with no physical effect
Legal duties to maintain workplaces

explanation: Good housekeeping is a foundational control for STF, fire load, and movement hazards.

distractorExplanations.c: Clutter can increase stress but physical STF remains primary.
distractorExplanations.d: Housekeeping supports legal workplace maintenance duties.

prompt: Biological risk from needlestick injury in a portering role is managed by:

options.text: Recapping needles by hand before disposal
Carrying loose sharps in pockets for speed
Sharps containers at point of use, training, reporting, and post-exposure prophylaxis pathway
Discouraging reporting to reduce paperwork

explanation: Needlestick protocols emphasise no recapping, sharps bins, and rapid medical assessment.

distractorExplanations.b: Pocket carry risks injury to porter and others.

prompt: LEV airflow indicator (gauge) helps operators by:

options.text: Decorating the workshop only
Replacing face fit testing for RPE
Measuring outdoor rainfall
Showing whether airflow is adequate before starting dusty work

explanation: Indicators warn when extraction fails, prompting stop-work until maintenance.

distractorExplanations.a: Aesthetic value is not the safety function.
distractorExplanations.b: Face fit testing is separate from LEV airflow.
distractorExplanations.c: Face fit testing is separate from LEV airflow.

prompt: A risk assessment for roof work identifies fragile materials and no edge protection. The decision should be:

options.text: Do not proceed until higher-order controls are implemented or work method changed
Proceed if workers sign a disclaimer
Use disclaimer and shorter lunch
Proceed Friday only

explanation: Disclaimers do not transfer legal duties; inadequate controls mean work should not start.

distractorExplanations.b: This option does not best address the scenario described.
distractorExplanations.c: Lunch length does not fix fall risk.
distractorExplanations.d: Day of week does not change fragility.

prompt: Integrating welfare with hazard control on a hot chemical process line means:

options.text: Locating drinking water only inside the bund of corrosive tanks
Providing hydration, cool rest areas, chemical hygiene, and maintained toilets away from the process
Removing rest to maximise throughput without heat plan
Combining toilet and chemical mixing room

explanation: Welfare must be accessible and hygienic, separated from chemical process zones.

distractorExplanations.a: Water inside corrosive bund risks contamination and exposure.
distractorExplanations.d: Toilets must not share space with mixing hazards.
